metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Competitive Agreeableness (31st pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Highly conscientious Conscientiousness (90th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Extraverted Extraversion (70th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (87th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly open Openness to Experience (81st pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (32nd pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Very High Achievement Striving (89th pct) — Ambitious, driven, works hard toward goals
  Very High Self-Discipline (94th pct) — Self-disciplined, persistent, finishes tasks
  High Activity Level (61st pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  High Excitement Seeking (72nd pct) — Thrill-seeking, excitement-hungry
  High Positive Emotions (74th pct) — Joyful, enthusiastic, optimistic
  Very High Anger / Hostility (80th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  Very High Self-Consciousness (84th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (81st pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Low Chronological Age (36th pct)

message.reasoning:
We are simulating a baseline opinion on "Defense Spending Increase Support". The profile: age 36, sex "Other", high conscientiousness (89.6), high neuroticism (87.1), high openness (80.9), moderately high extraversion (70), low agreeableness (30.7). Traits: competitive, skeptical, tough-minded, ambitious, disciplined, extraverted but anxious and hostile. High openness to ideas and values (84.8), but low trust (26.3). This person is likely to be skeptical of large government spending, especially on defense? But also high conscientiousness might support order and security. High neuroticism suggests anxiety about threats, possibly supporting defense spending? But low agreeableness and low trust suggest distrust of institutions? High openness to values (84.8) suggests they might question traditional defense priorities. They are not strongly ideological from the given traits. Need to produce a plausible score that is not extreme, and not a round number.

Given the combination: high neuroticism (anxiety, hostility) might make them worry about security threats, but low trust and high openness might make them skeptical of military expansion. Also high conscientiousness with high deliberation and duty might favor responsible spending but not necessarily increases. I'll aim for a moderate leaning against increase, around -200 to -400. Let's say -273.

Confidence: 0.73.

Reasoning: This individual's low agreeableness and low trust combine with high openness to values to produce skepticism of increased military spending, while high neuroticism fuels some security concerns but not enough to overcome distrust; conscientiousness supports fiscal discipline.
